title: Pick the semver bump from the observed contract change
description: Derive major/minor/patch from whether the public surface broke, grew, or only got fixed, not from gut feel or release cadence.
body: Use when deciding the next version number for a published library or service API. Do not bump major for an internal refactor that kept the contract, and avoid a patch bump when an exported signature, default, or wire format actually changed. Actions: diff the public surface (exports, types, CLI flags, response schema) against the last tag, classify each delta as break/add/fix, and choose major for any break, minor for additive-only, patch for fixes; record the classifying diff in the release notes. Validation signals include an API-diff tool report and a consumer contract test passing against the new version. Risk boundary: if a change might break downstream consumers and you cannot confirm, treat it as major and escalate rather than shipping a silent break under a patch.
